raw_question: In welchem Maß erhöht steigende Wohnkosten-Unbezahlbarkeit ursächlich die Unterstützung für rechtspopulistische / Anti-Establishment-Parteien in europäischen Städten, 2015–2025?
normalized_question: What is the causal effect of rising housing-cost unaffordability on subsequent support for separately classified right-populist and anti-establishment parties among residents of European cities from 2015 to 2025?
population: urban residents, with renters and owners analysed separately
comparator: otherwise comparable units with lower or no increase in unaffordability
outcome: individual vote choice or local party-family vote share at the next election
time_horizon: next election after exposure change within 2015-2025
geography: European cities subject to data and identification constraints

alternative_readings.label: individual_preference_change
alternative_readings.question: Does a household-level increase in housing-cost burden change the same individual’s later party support?
alternative_readings.material_difference: Requires individual longitudinal data and separates preference change from population composition.

alternative_readings.label: aggregate_city_change
alternative_readings.question: Does a city-level increase in housing-cost burden precede a change in party-family vote share?
alternative_readings.material_difference: Can use city panels but is vulnerable to ecological inference and residential sorting.
